description: Grammar-in-context explanations must be non-empty plain text (no HTML) and pass the safety check. Outputs are representative learner-level explanations.

cases.name: phrasal-verb

input.context: I happened to run into an old friend at the station.

cases.modelOutput: "Run into" is a phrasal verb that means to meet someone unexpectedly. In this sentence it shows a chance meeting at the station. For example: I ran into my teacher at the supermarket.

cases.name: idiom

input.context: She told a joke to break the ice at the meeting.

cases.modelOutput: "Break the ice" is an idiom. It means to do or say something to make people feel relaxed at the start of a social situation. For example: A quick game can break the ice on the first day of class.

cases.name: collocation

input.context: We need to make a decision before Friday.

cases.modelOutput: "Make a decision" is a common collocation, meaning to decide something. We say "make" rather than "do" with the word decision. For example: It is hard to make a decision when you are tired.
